threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look baffled when a double-decker bus zooms past your head at the stop. Locals just know to step back.
Last night, the 37 bus was packed with a silent dance party. Two guys in the back were belting out 90s hits like it was the last train home.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard someone casually discussing the best kebab shops while waiting for the tram at Karl-Marx-Straße, I felt at home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can spot an impromptu rave in a park, complete with sound systems, while kids are just a few meters away on a playground. Only in Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name the exact spot of the last Mission taco truck closing, and it’s not the one you’d expect.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus stopping mid-block yesterday to let a guy chase after his hat. The driver just smiled and waited. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think this area is all about Gaudí, but the real charm lies in the little plazas where locals gather for vermut, not the big crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by their utter confusion over the metro's color-coded map; locals know the shortcuts through the labyrinth.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still refer to the Mare Street traffic lights as 'that roundabout' instead of just knowing the chaos around them.
Caught the N37 last week—saw a guy moonwalk along the aisle. Whole bus erupted in laughter like it was a show. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the guy juggling flaming torches at Hermannplatz while a group of kids cheered him on. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find spontaneous parties in the U-Bahn, complete with DJs and dancing, as if the train is just a giant club on wheels.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they still call it 'The Mission' and not just 'Mission District' like the techies from the East Bay.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Nothing captures Muni like that moment when the bus is full, but the driver still stops to let on the guy with the accordion. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals drink wine at every meal, but trust me, you'll see more cerveses in Gràcia's terraces than any fancy bottle.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle to find the right 'pa amb tomàquet' at the corner bars; locals know it’s not just a menu item.
